chief_complaint: A 32-year-old man presented with acute chest pain and repeated vomiting following dinner.
history: The patient had a history of ulcerative colitis (UC) and reported a sensation of food impaction 2 hours after symptom onset. He presented to the emergency department with acute chest pain and repeated vomiting.
exam: Examination revealed subcutaneous emphysema and abdominal tenderness. Vital signs were not provided.
labs: Imaging with oral contrast-enhanced computed tomography revealed pneumomediastinum, pneumoperitoneum, and a distal esophageal perforation.
